data_quality_alert.detail: All seven daily_collector_findings contain legitimate macroeconomic reporting (Fed policy, ECB statements, tariff data, bond yields). Zero findings exhibit characteristics of foreign information manipulation operations, cognitive warfare, disinformation campaigns, or state actor FIMI tradecraft. Data appears misfiled to FIMI attribution monitor.
data_quality_alert.sources_verified: Deloitte Insights (Tier 2), Richmond Fed (Tier 1) — both legitimate financial/policy reporting.
data_quality_alert.recommendation: Route to Global Macro Monitor (GMM) or Economic Risk Monitor (ERM). Verify data pipeline integrity. Do not attempt FIMI attribution on legitimate economic reporting; creates false actor signals.

analyst_briefing: No FIMI campaigns, actor postures, or foreign information operations detected in this dataset. The seven daily collector findings constitute standard macroeconomic intelligence: Fed rate hold with divided member forecasts; ECB unchanged citing Middle East energy risks; 5-year breakeven inflation rates elevated; effective US tariff rate at 11.4% (highest since 1943); Powell on energy uncertainty; Fed funds futures pricing 60% no-cut probability; and cross-central-bank energy shock consensus. These are legitimate policy outputs and market reactions, not manipulation operations. No attribution analysis is warranted. This dataset has been misfiled to the FIMI/Cognitive Warfare Monitor and should be rerouted to the Global Macro Monitor or Economic Risk Monitor for proper analysis. No contested findings, actor posture changes, or cross-monitor FIMI escalation flags are applicable.
